service.name: Telegram Bot API
service.description_for_ai: Create bots that send messages, handle commands, and interact with Telegram users. 950M+ users. Free, fast, no rate limits for personal bots. The simplest way to build a conversational interface. Use to send notifications, create interactive bots, receive commands from users via Telegram.

auth.how_to_get: Message @BotFather on Telegram → /newbot → get token instantly. Free forever.

capabilities.description_for_ai: Send a text message to a Telegram user or group. Supports markdown formatting. Use to notify users, send alerts, or respond in a bot conversation.

chat_id.description: User ID, group ID, or @username. E.g. 123456789 or @mychannel

parse_mode.description: Markdown, MarkdownV2, or HTML for formatting

capabilities.description_for_ai: Get new messages sent to the bot (long polling). Use to receive user commands and messages without setting up a webhook.

offset.description: Update ID to start from (for pagination)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

capabilities.description_for_ai: Send a photo to a Telegram chat. Can be a URL or uploaded file.

photo.description: URL of image or file_id of previously uploaded photo

agent_hints.botfather: To create a bot: open Telegram → search @BotFather → send /newbot → choose name → get token. Takes 30 seconds.
agent_hints.chat_id_tip: To get your own chat_id: send any message to your bot, then call getUpdates — your ID is in message.from.id.
agent_hints.markdown_v2: MarkdownV2 requires escaping special chars with backslash: . ! - = + | { } # ( ) [ ]. Use HTML mode instead for simplicity.
agent_hints.webhook_vs_polling: For production use webhook (set once, Telegram pushes updates). For development use getUpdates polling.
agent_hints.russia_note: Telegram is very popular in Russia and CIS — great alternative to SMS for Russian-speaking users.
